evidence_scope: Existing hardware/capacity rows support training as a build-time compute problem, not a price meter.
reader_takeaway: Training tokens help create the model artifact; they are not the same economic event as every later user request.
visual_treatment: left-side build loop with one-way artifact arrow
blocked_claims: training cost; exact FLOPs; dataset size; model quality; energy per run

evidence_scope: Provider pricing rows expose input tokens as one visible unit of sale when cutoff status and scope are labeled.
reader_takeaway: The prompt, retrieved context, tool instructions, and conversation history are billable/load-bearing input.
visual_treatment: labeled token lane in the meter
blocked_claims: live price; exact provider margin; exact user spend; price-quality superiority

evidence_scope: Some provider rows expose cached-input pricing, but cache writes and conditions vary by provider.
reader_takeaway: Caching can change the visible meter, but it is not a universal discount or margin proof.
visual_treatment: parallel cached lane with caveat tag
blocked_claims: guaranteed savings; production cache hit rate; provider margin; universal availability

evidence_scope: Provider rows commonly expose output-token prices; reasoning/thinking tokens can complicate this unit.
reader_takeaway: Output often costs more than input at the listed meter, but exact economics depend on provider, model, scope, and date.
visual_treatment: output lane ending in answer stream
blocked_claims: live price; cost per answer; profitability; universal price ratio

evidence_scope: Long-context assets and pricing rows support context as capacity and scope, not as free memory.
reader_takeaway: Longer context can increase the amount of input the system must carry, route, cache, or price.
visual_treatment: expanding context box feeding input meter
blocked_claims: exact memory cost; product superiority; long-context usefulness; universal accuracy gain

evidence_scope: Reasoning assets support extra inference-time work as a schematic axis, not a measured production policy.
reader_takeaway: Some tasks buy more search, samples, verifier loops, or tool calls; that spends latency and compute before the answer.
blocked_claims: exact hidden budget; production routing; solved reasoning; cost curve

evidence_scope: Existing serving/economics visuals support latency as a user-visible constraint shaped by routing, batching, scheduling, and physical capacity.
reader_takeaway: Faster answers, cheaper batch jobs, and deeper reasoning are different service promises.
blocked_claims: exact latency; SLA; workload volume; routine dispatchability

evidence_scope: Routing appears as a schematic lever across economics and reasoning packages.
reader_takeaway: Providers can expose or hide choices among fast path, think path, tool path, batch path, and escalation.
blocked_claims: actual production behavior; quality guarantee; autonomy; savings

evidence_scope: Pricing rows include batch tiers, but batch changes latency and product scope.
reader_takeaway: Batch can lower visible price only when the user accepts a different service contract.
visual_treatment: separate delayed lane, not merged with standard price
blocked_claims: standard-price comparison; live price; universal savings; equivalent latency

evidence_scope: Existing levers and price-quality audit support distillation/smaller-model choice as a lever, not as a proven frontier.
reader_takeaway: Cheaper models can be useful in routed systems, but price alone is not quality.
blocked_claims: price-quality frontier; broad superiority; customer ROI; adoption

evidence_scope: Existing Chapter 22 route-to-market visual separates product surfaces.
reader_takeaway: The same model family can be sold through subscriptions, API meters, enterprise bundles, and cloud partnerships.
blocked_claims: revenue; paid seats; active use; productivity; margin

evidence_scope: Blocker rows make margin and marginal-cost claims visible as unsourced unless same-scope cost evidence exists.
reader_takeaway: The meter is public before the margin is knowable.
blocked_claims: provider margin; cost per token; revenue; profitability; capex allocation; utilization
